steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content: The strongest clue: scientists theorized about phlogiston to explain combustion, and Priestley's naming of oxygen as “dephlogisticated air” reveals he was interpreting the discovery within that same framework.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
”For this reason” establishes the causal relationship: the phlogiston theory explains why Priestley named the newly discovered gas within that framework.

The other three don't establish causation—”In other words” suggests restatement, while “Alternatively” and “Nevertheless” signal opposition or contrast rather than consequence.

That matches Choice B — For this reason,.
